anāvattidhammā.meaning: not liable to return; by nature not coming back (to this world)
breakdown: (At the end of a compound) having the nature of, liable to.

anāvaṭadvāratāya.meaning: by keeping the door not shut; the state of having an open door (hospitality)
anāvaṭadvāratāya.gram: feminine abstract compound noun, instrumental singular

anūhate.meaning: not removed, not taken out, not destroyed
anūhate.gram: past participle of na ūhanati (√han), locative singular

anūpaghāto.gram: masculine noun (from na upaghāteti), nominative singular
breakdown: Negative prefix "not" (before a vowel, with lengthened ū).

anūpavādo.meaning: not finding fault, not complaining; non-blaming
anūpavādo.gram: masculine noun (from na upavadati), nominative singular
breakdown: Negative prefix "not" (before a vowel, with lengthened ū).

apabodhati.meaning: to render oneself conspicuous; to keep alert
apabodhati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present (apa + √budh, irregular causative)

apaccanīkapaṭipadaṁ.meaning: the practice that is not contrary; walking the path without going against (the teaching)

apaciyati.meaning: to be esteemed, to be honoured
breakdown: Prefix (here in the sense of reverence).

apacāyamāno.gram: present participle of apacāyati, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Prefix (here in the sense of reverence).

apalāpāyaṁ.meaning: this one (is) without prattle; free of idle talk
apalāpāyaṁ.gram: adjective + demonstrative pronoun (a + palāpa + ayaṁ, fused by sandhi)

aparappaccayo.meaning: not dependent on others; having confidence grounded in oneself (in the Dhamma seen for oneself)
breakdown: Other, another. (The pp is sandhi doubling.)
aparappaccayo.note: divergence: the lexicon's gloss line reads "further + confidence"; the established sense of aparappaccaya, supported by DPD's analysis (na + para + paccaya), is "not relying on others" — having direct confidence independent of another's word.

apare.meaning: others; "by others" — the formula with which the commentators introduce diverging interpretations
apare.note: divergence: the lexicon notes that commentators collect diverging views introduced with apare ("others [say]"), e.g. the varying interpretations of the stages of the 5000 years in the Anāgatavaṁsa.

aparihāniye.meaning: not connected with decay or loss; (conditions) leading to no decline
aparihāniye.summary: Used of the aparihāniyā dhammā, the conditions of non-decline taught in the Mahāparinibbāna-sutta.
aparihāniye.gram: adjective, locative singular or accusative plural

aparisuddhasīlo.meaning: whose sīla is not completely purified; of impure morality
aparisuddhasīlo.goenka: Sīla, morality, is the indispensable foundation of the path in the tradition: only on the basis of pure conduct can samādhi and paññā develop. One who is aparisuddhasīla — whose sīla is not completely purified — lacks this foundation; hence the emphasis on scrupulous observance of the precepts as the starting point of meditation practice.

apassaṁ.gram: negated present participle of passati (√dis), nominative singular

apaṇṇakappaṭipadaṁ.meaning: the certain path, the true (unmistaken) way of practice
apaṇṇakappaṭipadaṁ.gram: feminine abstract compound noun, accusative singular
breakdown: Path, way of practice. (The pp is sandhi doubling at the compound seam.)

apesayī.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of peseti/pesayati

apeto.gram: past participle of apeti (apa + √i), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root "to go" + past participle suffix -ta: "gone away from".

apetteyyatā.meaning: disrespect towards one's father; absence of filial duty to the father
breakdown: Dutiful, respectful towards one's father (from pitar).

aphāsukakāmā.meaning: wishing discomfort; desiring (another's) trouble and uneasiness

aphāsuññeva.gram: noun aphāsu, accusative singular + emphatic particle yeva (fused by sandhi)
breakdown: Emphatic particle "just, only" (ṁ + y fuse to -ññ-).

appaccayañca.gram: noun/adjective appaccaya, accusative singular + conjunction ca
breakdown: = ca "and", with sandhi ṁ + c → ñc.

appahontassa.meaning: of what does not suffice; of one for whom (the remainder) is too little
appahontassa.gram: present participle of na pahoti, genitive/dative singular
breakdown: Sufficing, being enough (present participle of pahoti, pa + √hū).
appahontassa.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line reads "little + of what remains" (appa + hontassa); DPD analyses the form as the negated present participle of pahoti "to suffice". Both point to insufficiency of what remains.

breakdown: Duty, task, what is to be done (√kar).

appamattakaṁ.meaning: of little measure, trifling; of rare occurrence

appamatto.gram: past participle of na pamajjati, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Negligent, heedless (past participle of pamajjati, pa + √mad).
appamatto.goenka: Appamatta, heedful, is the quality of one established in appamāda — diligence, non-negligence. The Buddha's final exhortation, appamādena sampādetha ("strive on diligently"), is repeatedly emphasised in the tradition: the meditator is to work patiently and persistently, with unremitting awareness, never letting heedlessness interrupt the practice.

appamattā.gram: past participle of na pamajjati, plural

appamattā diligently appamatto.gram: past participle of na pamajjati (plural appamattā, singular appamatto)
appamattā diligently appamatto.note: uncertain: this key carries the English word "diligently" over from the source glossary's layout; the Pāli entries are appamattā (plural) and appamatto (singular), "heedful, diligent".

appamādena.summary: Familiar from the Buddha's last words: appamādena sampādetha, "strive on diligently".
appamādena.gram: masculine abstract noun appamāda, instrumental singular
appamādena.goenka: Appamāda, non-negligence or diligence, carries the weight of the Buddha's final exhortation: appamādena sampādetha — strive on diligently. In the tradition this is the call to work patiently and persistently, maintaining the continuity of awareness in every posture and activity, since heedlessness allows the old habit pattern of reaction to reassert itself.

appamādādhikaraṇaṁ.meaning: in consequence of diligence; on account of vigilance and zeal
breakdown: In consequence of, on account of (fused by vowel sandhi to -dādhi-).

appanigghosañca.meaning: and with little noise; and free of shouting and voices
appanigghosañca.gram: adjective, compound, accusative singular + conjunction ca
breakdown: = ca "and", with sandhi ṁ + c → ñc.

appannapānabhojane.meaning: in little food, drink and nourishment; (a household) with scant food and drink
breakdown: Little, few (with junction nasal before anna).

appanā.meaning: fixed application of the mind; (in the lexicon's gloss) thought, reasoning
breakdown: Stem of appeti "to fix on, apply" (√ar).

appanāppattaṁ.meaning: having reached full application of mind; attained to fixed thought or resolve
appanāppattaṁ.gram: compound: appanā + patta (past participle of pāpuṇāti), accusative singular
breakdown: Application of mind, fixed thought, resolve.
Attained, reached (past participle of pāpuṇāti; the pp is sandhi doubling).

apparajakkhajātikā.meaning: beings with little dust (of defilement) in their eyes; those of a nature only slightly obscured
apparajakkhajātikā.summary: From the Brahmā Sahampati episode: beings "with little dust in their eyes" who are able to understand the Dhamma.
breakdown: Having dust (raja "dust, defilement" + akkha "eye"; the kk is sandhi doubling).
Of such a clan or nature; beings of that kind.

appasannānañceva.meaning: and just for those who are not (yet) favourably disposed; for the unconverted
appasannānañceva.gram: adjective appasanna, genitive plural + particles ca + eva
breakdown: Pleased, favourably disposed, having faith (past participle of pasīdati).

appassakā.meaning: having little of one's own; possessing little
breakdown: Little, few. (The ss is sandhi doubling.)

appattassa.meaning: of what has not been attained, reached or gained
appattassa.gram: past participle of na pāpuṇāti, genitive/dative singular
